notes.note: Tutor and secundus heres: under Roman testamentary practice, a father could appoint a guardian (tutor) to manage his minor son's affairs, and a second heir to succeed should the principal heir die or refuse. Cicero's own appointment to both posts marks the depth of Pinnius's confidence in him.
Sestertium octogies: literally "eighty hundred-thousands" of sesterces, i.e. 8,000,000 HS --- a very substantial debt, of the order of an equestrian's full property qualification eight times over.

Fides dignitasque: the "honour" Cicero invokes is fides --- the magistrate's good faith and the trust placed in him; dignitas is the proconsul's standing. Cicero is asking Silius to press the Nicaeans hard, but only as far as his role permits, not beyond it.
